title: Caution-Regime Proxy Features
summary: Official F1/FIA material shows pit decisions change sharply when Safety Car or VSC windows open and when a stop becomes effectively cheaper.
rationale: Official F1/FIA material shows pit decisions change sharply when Safety Car or VSC windows open and when a stop becomes effectively cheaper. The table lacks an explicit caution flag, so race-wide synchronous pace shocks are likely a missing latent state.
implementation_hint: Create race-lap aggregates on observables only: median lap time, median lap delta, IQR, share with very large positive lap deltas, share pitting this lap, and lap-to-lap changes in those aggregates. Add lagged shock/restart flags and interact them with tyre-life pressure, compound, and laps remaining.
expected_effect: Should recover a hidden regime that explains clustered stops and improve generalization on races with interruptions or restart windows.
risk: Field aggregates can be noisy; ensure they use only current-lap observable columns and not any future-derived signal.
